recaps.text: In an earlier journey the hobbit Bilbo Baggins found a plain gold ring that makes its wearer invisible, taking it from the creature Gollum deep beneath the mountains, and kept it for many years. Unknown to him, it is no ordinary ring but the One Ring, forged in secret by the Dark Lord Sauron to rule all the others and long lost to him. Now Sauron is rising again in the east, and he wants his ring back.

At his birthday party Bilbo vanishes with the ring and leaves the Shire, passing it and Bag End to his heir Frodo. The wizard Gandalf, long suspicious, returns to confirm the terrible truth: the ring is the One Ring, and the Enemy is hunting for it. He warns Frodo that he must carry it away from the Shire before dark riders track it to his home.

Frodo sets out with Sam, Merry, and Pippin, pursued by shadowy Black Riders. After perils in the Old Forest and the Barrow-downs, and help from the strange Tom Bombadil, they reach the village of Bree and fall in with Strider, a Ranger who agrees to guide them toward Rivendell. On a lonely hilltop the Riders attack and Frodo is stabbed with a cursed blade. Gravely ill, he is carried onward and, at a river crossing, an elf-lady's sudden flood sweeps the Riders away just as he reaches safety.

Frodo wakes healed in Rivendell, reunited with Bilbo and Gandalf. At the Council of Elrond the ring's history is told and its threat weighed; since it cannot be safely used or hidden, they resolve that it must be carried into Mordor and destroyed in the fire where it was made. Frodo offers to bear it, and a Fellowship of nine is chosen to go with him - Frodo and his three hobbit friends, Gandalf, Aragorn, Boromir of Gondor, and the elf Legolas and dwarf Gimli.

Turned back by snow in the mountains, the Fellowship passes through the dark mines of Moria, where Gandalf falls into shadow battling a demon of fire on a narrow bridge. Grieving, the rest reach Lothlórien and the Lady Galadriel, who shelters and counsels them before they journey on by river. There Boromir is overcome by longing for the ring and tries to take it from Frodo. Frightened by what it can do even to good men, Frodo decides to go on toward Mordor alone - but Sam refuses to be left behind, and the two slip away together as the company is scattered.
